summary: Determinism harnesses for applyFilterOrSearch only exercise the no-op AppliedFilter, so the universal-determinism property is not actually witnessed for non-trivial filter / search / sort cases.
defect: PROP-002 (REQ-015) is stated as `∀ (Feed, AppliedFilter, NoteFileSnapshot[]): applyFilterOrSearch(...) deepEquals applyFilterOrSearch(...)`. The harness at prop-002-apply-determinism.harness.test.ts lines 22-39 binds the AppliedFilter to `arbAppliedFilterNoOp()` only — empty tags, empty frontmatterFields, null query. The same is true for PROP-007 (sort determinism) at prop-007-sort-determinism.harness.test.ts lines 22-40. Neither harness ever generates a case where criteria.tags is non-empty, frontmatterFields is non-empty, or query is non-null. The natural mis-implementation that PROP-002/PROP-007 are meant to catch — e.g., a comparator with non-deterministic tiebreak hidden behind the filter or search branch, or a filter with hidden state across calls — is not actually exercised by the property test. The Tier-1 claim of ≥1000 runs (PROP-002) and ≥200 runs (PROP-007) is therefore over-claimed because the input space the harness actually samples is degenerate.
evidence: prop-002-apply-determinism.harness.test.ts line 24: `arbAppliedFilterNoOp()` is the only AppliedFilter generator used. The same generator is used in PROP-007 at prop-007-sort-determinism.harness.test.ts line 25 and line 47. By contrast, PROP-009 at prop-009-has-zero-results-flag.harness.test.ts line 41 correctly includes `arbAppliedFilterWithTags()` in addition to the no-op variant, demonstrating that a richer arbitrary is available.
suggested_fix: Add a second test in each of prop-002 and prop-007 that uses arbAppliedFilterWithTags() (already defined in _arbitraries.ts), and ideally a third that varies query and sortOrder.direction. Even 200 runs over the non-trivial generators would be enough to make the determinism claim meaningful. The existing tests can stay as they are.
